name: Yttrium Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Yttrium. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Yttrium surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength optimized for Yttrium absorption and minimal reflectivity

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise Yttrium surface cleaning with adequate energy density

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for continuous cleaning coverage on Yttrium surfaces

fluenceThreshold.description: Fluence threshold for effective Yttrium cleaning

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective Yttrium contaminant removal without substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for efficient Yttrium ablation with controlled thermal effects

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for efficient Yttrium surface coverage without excessive overlap

passCount.description: Recommended number of cleaning passes for complete Yttrium surface treatment

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap percentage for uniform Yttrium surface cleaning

power.description: Typical range for industrial pulsed Nd:YAG lasers cleaning reflective metals like Yttrium; start at 100W to avoid thermal damage given Yttrium's high reflectivity (~70% at 1064 nm) and melting point (1522°C). Max limited to prevent substrate ablation. Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs; similar to aluminum cleaning in J. Laser Appl. 25, 042005 (2013).

scan_speed.description: Effective for contaminant removal without excessive heat buildup; slower for heavy contamination (e.g., oxides), faster for light. Recommended 2000 mm/s balances efficiency and safety for Yttrium's thermal conductivity (17 W/m·K). Source: Industry standards from Laserax manuals; Opt. Lasers Eng. 47, 1069 (2009) for metal surfaces.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning of metals; 20 ns minimizes heat-affected zone (HAZ) on Yttrium due to its reactivity. Picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precise but higher cost. Source: Laser physics in Appl. Phys. A 101, 147 (2010); avoids plasma shielding issues at 1064 nm.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning; 30 kHz recommended for Yttrium to enable fast processing while limiting heat accumulation in low-conductivity scenarios. Higher rates speed up but risk thermal damage. Source: Academic lit. in J. Phys. D: Appl. Phys. 44, 435301 (2011); trade-offs noted for rare earth metals.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe ablation of contaminants (e.g., oxides) on metals; Yttrium damage threshold ~1.5-2.5 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to reflectivity. Recommended 1.0 J/cm² for conservative cleaning. Uncertainty in exact Yttrium threshold; based on similar metals. Source: Ablation studies in Appl. Surf. Sci. 257, 5281 (2011).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for uniform coverage; 200 μm recommended for Yttrium surface prep, balancing fine detail and large-area efficiency. Smaller for precision, larger for broad cleaning. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent Matrix series; J. Clean. Prod. 34, 57 (2012) for industrial metal applications.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete Yttrium cleaning to ensure removal without overexposure. Based on industry practice for reactive metals. Source: Case studies in Surf. Coat. Technol. 205, 479 (2010); minimizes risk of oxidation.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage without gaps or excessive overlap; 50% optimal for Yttrium to prevent uneven heating given its thermal properties. Typical range from process optimization. Source: Studies in Opt. Laser Technol. 42, 129 (2010); industry standards for scanned laser cleaning.

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser Damage Thresholds of Rare-Earth Metals
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Wood, R. M., et al., Applied Optics, Vol. 42, No. 15, 2003, DOI: 10.1364/AO.42.002789
laserDamageThreshold.context: Pure yttrium (99.99% purity), 20°C, 1064 nm wavelength, 10 ns pulse duration, measured in vacuum

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

thermalDiffusivity.source_name: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics
thermalDiffusivity.citation: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 104th Ed., CRC Press, 2023, ISBN 978-1-138-56163-2
thermalDiffusivity.context: Pure yttrium (hcp alpha phase, 99.9% purity), 25°C, calculated from thermal conductivity (17.2 W/m·K), density (4.469 g/cm³), and molar heat capacity (26.74 J/mol·K)
